title: pod: honor --infra-command
body:
if --infra-command is specified, it must have a higher precedence over
the command specified in the image.

If the image specifies an entrypoint, it must be used also with
--infra-command.

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *giuseppe* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Concern: I think this may unconditionally override image entrypoints if the config file does not explicitly set `infra_command = ""` (otherwise, the defaults will plug in `"/pause"`)

> Concern: I think this may unconditionally override image entrypoints if the config file does not explicitly set `infra_command = ""` (otherwise, the defaults will plug in `"/pause"`)

good point.  Should we default to ""?
As long as this doesn't break the default k8s.io pause image, I think that's a good idea

> As long as this doesn't break the default k8s.io pause image, I think that's a good idea

it seems the k8s.io image has an ENTRYPOINT defined: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/master/build/pause/Dockerfile

> As long as this doesn't break the default k8s.io pause image, I think that's a good idea

PR opened here: https://github.com/containers/common/pull/41

the correct thing to do would be to honor the setting in this order:

- the `--infra-command` value
- what is set in the image
- the default setting in the conf file.

I'll rework it and open a new PR
